task: I’m trying to make a simple checklist for what to do if the house loses power for more than a few hours and a couple of other things go wrong at the same time. Can you help me lay it out in the right order so I don’t miss anything important?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a simple **in-order checklist** you can use when the house loses power for more than a few hours and other problems are happening too.

## 1) Safety first
- [ ] Check for immediate danger: smoke, fire, gas smell, sparking, flooding, fallen wires, or a breaker panel issue
- [ ] If you smell gas, see sparks, or there’s fire/flooding: leave if needed and call emergency services
- [ ] Keep away from downed power lines
- [ ] Turn off or unplug sensitive electronics if it’s safe to do so

## 2) Confirm the outage
- [ ] Check whether the outage is just your house or the whole area
- [ ] Look at the main breaker panel if safe
- [ ] Check utility outage map / call power company
- [ ] Write down the time the power went out

## 3) Protect food and medicine
- [ ] Keep refrigerator and freezer doors closed
- [ ] Move critical medicine to a cooler if needed
- [ ] If you use medical equipment, switch to backup power or call for help immediately
- [ ] Decide what food must be used first

## 4) Make a quick priority list of the other problems
- [ ] List each issue that is happening at the same time
- [ ] Mark which ones are:
  - [ ] urgent safety issue
  - [ ] time-sensitive
  - [ ] can wait
  - [ ] just needs documentation

## 5) Contact the right people in the right order
- [ ] Call emergency services first if there is danger
- [ ] Call the utility company about the outage
- [ ] Call landlord / property manager / repair service if the house equipment is affected
- [ ] Call insurance if there is damage, flooding, or spoiled property
- [ ] Call anyone else involved only after the urgent items are handled

## 6) Document everything
- [ ] Write down date and time of each problem
- [ ] Note what happened and where
- [ ] Take photos or video if safe
- [ ] Save names, phone numbers, and reference numbers from every call
- [ ] Record what you were told and any promised follow-up

## 7) Preserve evidence and receipts
- [ ] Keep damaged items
- [ ] Save receipts for food, batteries, hotel, repairs, or supplies
- [ ] Don’t throw anything away until you know whether it needs to be inspected

## 8) Set the next action
- [ ] Decide what must happen today
- [ ] Decide what can wait until power returns
- [ ] Set a reminder to follow up if no one calls back

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page printable checklist with checkboxes** in a very plain format, like the letter checklist you asked for before.

user_simulator.text: Pretty good, but it’s still a little generic. I’d want it tighter and more practical — like, what if the power is out and the fridge is warming up, but also a tree’s down or a pipe is leaking. Put the truly urgent stuff first, then the rest. And I’d rather have it in a plain checklist I can print, not all the extra explanation.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter, more practical **plain checklist** in priority order.

---

# Power Out + Other Problems Checklist

## 1) Immediate danger
- [ ] Check for fire, smoke, gas smell, sparks, flooding, or downed wires
- [ ] If there is danger, leave the area if needed and call emergency services
- [ ] Stay away from downed power lines
- [ ] If water is near outlets, wiring, or the breaker panel, stay clear

## 2) Stop the worst damage
- [ ] If safe, turn off water to a leaking pipe
- [ ] If safe, shut off electricity to the affected area
- [ ] If safe, unplug sensitive electronics
- [ ] Close refrigerator and freezer doors
- [ ] Move food or medicine to a cooler or alternate cold storage if needed

## 3) Identify what is happening
- [ ] Write down each problem:
  - [ ] Power outage
  - [ ] Tree down
  - [ ] Leak / pipe burst
  - [ ] No heat / no AC
  - [ ] Food warming up
  - [ ] Other: __________
- [ ] Note the time each problem started
- [ ] Note whether it is getting worse

## 4) Call the right help
- [ ] Emergency services if there is immediate danger
- [ ] Utility company for the outage
- [ ] Electric company / power utility if wires or poles are involved
- [ ] Plumber if there is a leak or pipe burst
- [ ] Tree service / property manager / landlord if a tree is blocking access or damaging the house
- [ ] Insurance company if there is damage

## 5) Protect people, food, and property
- [ ] Keep everyone away from unsafe areas
- [ ] Move valuables away from water
- [ ] Keep a list of food that may spoil
- [ ] Save medication that needs refrigeration
- [ ] Use flashlights instead of candles

## 6) Document everything
- [ ] Take photos or video if safe
- [ ] Write down names, numbers, and reference numbers
- [ ] Record what each person told you
- [ ] Save receipts for repairs, food, batteries, and supplies

## 7) Follow up
- [ ] Check what must be done today
- [ ] Check what can wait until power returns
- [ ] Set reminders for calls back or repairs
- [ ] Recheck food, water, and damage before throwing anything away

---

If you want, I can turn this into a **single-page printable version with bigger checkboxes and no extra text**.

user_simulator.text:
Better. Still a little clean for real life, but close.

Make it even plainer and put the order like this:
1) danger to people
2) stop active damage
3) call the right person
4) protect food/papers/property
5) write it down
6) follow up

And don’t tuck the important bits under headings too much. I’d want lines I can just scan fast in the dark if I have to. Like:
- [ ] smell gas / see smoke / sparks / water near wiring = get out
- [ ] shut off water if a pipe is leaking
- [ ] close fridge/freezer right away
- [ ] call power company / plumber / tree service as needed
- [ ] take photos before anything gets moved

That kind of thing. If you can, strip it down to one page, no extra wording.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a stripped-down, plain version in the order you asked for:

# Emergency Checklist: Power Out + Other Problems

## 1) Danger to people
- [ ] smell gas / see smoke / sparks / fire = get out / call emergency services
- [ ] water near outlets / wiring / breaker panel = stay away
- [ ] downed power lines = stay away
- [ ] anyone hurt / trapped / unable to breathe = call emergency services

## 2) Stop active damage
- [ ] shut off water if a pipe is leaking
- [ ] shut off power to affected area if safe
- [ ] close fridge / freezer right away
- [ ] unplug electronics if safe
- [ ] move items away from water
- [ ] use flashlights, not candles

## 3) Call the right person
- [ ] power outage = call power company
- [ ] wires / poles down = call power company + emergency services if dangerous
- [ ] leaking pipe / burst pipe = call plumber
- [ ] tree down / blocking access / on house = call tree service / landlord / property manager
- [ ] damage to house / belongings = call insurance
- [ ] if unsure and it’s urgent = call emergency services

## 4) Protect food / papers / property
- [ ] keep fridge / freezer closed
- [ ] move food to cooler if needed
- [ ] save refrigerated medicine
- [ ] move papers / valuables away from water
- [ ] keep damaged items if they may need to be seen

## 5) Write it down
- [ ] write date / time power went out
- [ ] write date / time each problem started
- [ ] take photos / video before moving anything
- [ ] write who you called
- [ ] write name / time / reference number
- [ ] write what they told you

## 6) Follow up
- [ ] check what still needs action today
- [ ] check what can wait
- [ ] set reminder for callbacks
- [ ] recheck food / water / damage before throwing anything out

If you want, I can make this into an even tighter **printable one-page version with only checkboxes and no headings**.

user_simulator.text: That’s the one. Plain enough to use, and the order makes sense. I’d print that and keep it with the flashlight and the insurance papers.
